summary: September 2022 Cavallo customer newsletter teasing an upcoming promotion and sharing a story of a laminitic zebra mare rehabilitated with Cavallo hoof boots.
Explains how Cavallo hoof boots stay on via their patented conical shape and industrial Velcro, with fitting guidance for keeping boots secured.
Lists six ways Cavallo hoof boots protect horses from gravel, covering stone bruises, sensitivity, and rough-terrain pain for barefoot and shod horses.
Summarizes a Western Kentucky University study finding Cavallo Trek hoof boots provide hoof protection, traction, and reduced pressure versus metal shoes.
Argues for winterizing horses with Cavallo hoof boots and studs to prevent snow/ice ball-up, bruising, and injuries during cold-weather riding.
Describes how Cavallo hoof boots stay on in thick snow and freezing mud, detailing the conical design, TPU soles, leather padding, and break-in tips.
Tells the story of Romeo, a pony with insulin resistance and winter laminitis, and how Cavallo boots eased his cold-weather hoof pain.
Advises horse owners on handling hoof puncture wounds, including cleaning, packing, and using Cavallo boots to aid comfort during healing.
A personal narrative from Cavallo's president on transitioning away from her first farrier and taking ownership of her horse's hoof care.
In-depth guide to navicular disease in horses, covering early warning signs, causes, management, and how Cavallo boots support affected horses.
Explains how Cavallo hoof boots protect thin-soled and sensitive horses from painful surfaces, absorbing shock and providing immediate relief.
Launch announcement for the Trek Pro hoof boot, listing 15 reasons it improves on the Trek with a molded upper, sleek design, and special pricing.
